title: Add dev-only database clear endpoint
description:
## Goal
Add a way to clear the database during development/testing without manual file deletion.

## Requirements
1. Add POST /admin/clear-db endpoint that wipes all data
2. Gate behind //go:build dev build tag — endpoint only exists in dev builds
3. Add make dev target that runs with -tags dev
4. Update README with dev workflow

## Implementation
- Create new file with //go:build dev at top (e.g., handlers_dev.go)
- Endpoint clears all tables: paystubs, filing_status, tax_brackets cache
- Returns confirmation message

## Usage
- make dev — runs server with dev features
- make build or go build — production build without dev features

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Endpoint exists when built with -tags dev
- [ ] Endpoint returns 404 when built without dev tag
- [ ] Database cleared after calling endpoint
- [ ] make dev target works

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on PRs and pushes to main. Should run the same checks as 'make check': lint (golangci-lint), test (go test ./...), and build (go build ./...). Keep it simple — single workflow file, no caching or fancy optimizations needed initially.

title: Generalize paystub PDF parser for more paycheck formats
description: The PDF parser fails on some paystub formats. Need to generalize regex patterns to handle more payroll providers and layouts.

title: Fix upload button and add multi-file/drag-drop support
description: Upload & Parse button wasn't working due to HTMX not picking up programmatically-set files. Added multi-file support and drag-and-drop with visual feedback (green checkmarks for success, red X for errors).
close_reason: Upload button working with multi-file support, drag-drop, error display with red X marks, and Clear button

title: Support accurate estimates from a single paystub using YTD totals
description: Currently the calculations improve with more paystubs, but a user uploading one paystub in June should still get a reasonable estimate because that stub contains YTD cumulative totals (gross, federal withheld, deductions). We shouldn't require uploading all prior paystubs to get useful numbers. The YTD fields on a single stub give us everything we need to estimate annual income, current withholding rate, and remaining liability. More paystubs refine accuracy (pay frequency inference, bonus detection, trend analysis) but one should be enough for a solid baseline.

title: Hourly worker support for EOY projection
description:
## Goal
Support accurate EOY projections for hourly workers whose pay varies by hours worked.

## Background
Salaried workers have consistent paychecks, but hourly workers' withholding varies with hours. A short pay period shouldn't drag down the projection.

## Requirements
1. Extract hours worked from paystubs (for hourly workers)
2. Calculate rolling average of hours to determine "typical" pay period
3. Normalize withholding to typical hours before using for projection
4. Anomaly detection — exclude outlier weeks from rolling average:
   - Unusually short weeks (vacation, sick time)
   - Unusually long weeks (heavy overtime)

## Example
If typical is 80 hours but last paycheck was 60 hours with $800 withheld:
- Normalized withholding = $800 * (80/60) = $1,067
- Use $1,067 for projection, not $800

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Hours worked extracted from paystub
- [ ] Rolling average calculated correctly
- [ ] Anomaly detection excludes outlier weeks
- [ ] Withholding normalized to typical hours for projection

title: Extract pre-tax deductions from paystubs
description:
## Goal
Automatically extract pre-tax deductions from paystubs and account for them in tax calculations.

## Deductions to Extract
- 401(k) contributions
- 403(b) contributions
- HSA contributions
- FSA contributions (healthcare and dependent care)
- Commuter benefits

## Requirements
1. Update PDF parser to extract these deduction amounts from paystubs
2. Subtract pre-tax deductions from gross income when calculating tax liability
3. Track YTD contributions per deduction type
4. Fetch IRS contribution limits and cache per year in SQLite (same pattern as tax brackets)
   - 401(k)/403(b): $23,500 for 2025
   - HSA: $4,300 individual / $8,550 family for 2025
   - FSA: $3,300 for 2025
5. Warn when approaching limits (within 10%)

## Out of Scope
- Traditional IRA / Roth IRA (future follow-on)
- Recommendations for contribution optimization (future follow-on)

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Parser extracts 401k/403b contributions from sample fixture
- [ ] Parser extracts HSA/FSA contributions from sample fixture
- [ ] Tax calculation subtracts pre-tax deductions from gross
- [ ] YTD totals tracked per deduction type per person
- [ ] Contribution limits fetched and cached per year
- [ ] Warning displayed when within 10% of IRS limit

title: Add dark theme support
description:
## Goal
Add dark theme support to the web UI.

## Requirements
1. Default to system preference (prefers-color-scheme media query)
2. Add manual toggle in the header to override system preference
3. Persist user's choice (localStorage or cookie)
4. Theme should apply to all pages consistently

## Implementation Notes
- Use CSS custom properties (variables) for colors
- Toggle should be visible and accessible in header
- Consider sun/moon icon for toggle

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Dark theme applied when system is in dark mode
- [ ] Light theme applied when system is in light mode
- [ ] Toggle switches between themes
- [ ] Preference persisted across page reloads
- [ ] All pages styled correctly in both themes

title: EOY tax projection (core)
description:
## Goal
Calculate estimated total tax paid by end of year, using most recent paycheck to project remaining withholding.

## Requirements
1. Calculate total withheld-to-date from all uploaded paystubs
2. Use most recent regular paycheck's withholding to project remaining pay periods
3. Project per-person — each person's last paycheck projects their own remaining withholding
4. Sum both projections for combined household estimate
5. Display: (Your withheld + projected) + (Spouse withheld + projected) = estimated EOY total

## Pay Frequency Detection
- Infer pay frequency from paystub dates (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly)
- Calculate remaining pay periods from current date to end of year

## Bonus Detection
Detect bonus paychecks using multiple signals:
- Off-cycle date (not on regular pay schedule)
- Anomalous amount for salaried workers
- Different withholding pattern (flat 22% supplemental rate, no extra withholding)

When detected:
- Include bonus withholding in "already paid" total
- Exclude bonus from projection baseline (use most recent regular paycheck instead)

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Pay frequency inferred from paystub dates
- [ ] Remaining pay periods calculated correctly
- [ ] Projection calculated using most recent regular paycheck
- [ ] Bonus paychecks detected using multiple signals
- [ ] Bonus withholding included in "already paid" total
- [ ] Bonus excluded from projection baseline
- [ ] Separate projections per person (not mixed)
- [ ] Dashboard displays EOY estimate with breakdown

title: Research overtime tax rules and implement
description:
## Goal
Research new overtime tax break rules and implement them in the tax calculation.

## Background
There are reportedly new overtime rules with tax break implications. This needs research before implementation.

## Requirements
1. Research current federal overtime tax rules
2. Document findings in DECISIONS.md
3. Determine if/how overtime affects tax liability differently than regular pay
4. Implement any special handling needed for overtime withholding in projections

## Deliverables
- DECISIONS.md entry documenting the overtime tax rules
- Implementation if applicable
- Tests if implementation added

## Test Criteria
- [ ] DECISIONS.md updated with overtime tax research
- [ ] If applicable: overtime detected in paystubs
- [ ] If applicable: tax calculation handles overtime correctly

title: Dashboard link should persist after paystub upload
description:
## Goal
After uploading a paystub, the "Go to Dashboard" link should stay visible until the user navigates away.

## Problem
Currently the success message and dashboard link auto-fade too quickly after paystub upload. Users may not have time to click the link.

## Requirements
1. Remove auto-fade behavior for the dashboard link
2. Link should persist until user navigates to another page
3. Paystub preview can still clear if desired, but dashboard link stays

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Dashboard link visible after upload
- [ ] Link does not auto-fade
- [ ] Link remains until navigation

title: PDF parsing fails to extract required fields from some paystubs
description: Some PDF paystubs fail to parse with errors about missing required information. Need to investigate PDF structure and improve parsing robustness.
close_reason: Fixed parser to handle Rippling paystub format - PAID TO, table-style YTD values

title: Withholding calculator (TDD)
description: Tests first. Calculate combined tax liability from two incomes using cached brackets. Apply standard deduction based on filing status. Subtract total withheld-to-date from both earners. Recommend additional withholding for the higher earner. Determine remaining pay periods to spread additional withholding.

title: Project scaffolding
description: Go module init, directory structure, Makefile with build/test/lint targets, golangci-lint config, SQLite setup with migrations, configurable DB_PATH. This is the foundation — all other tasks depend on it.

title: PDF parsing (TDD)
description: Tests first. Upload endpoint for PDFs. Extract first name, gross pay, federal tax withheld, pay period dates, YTD totals. Strip ALL sensitive data (SSN, address, bank accounts). Clear errors for unparseable/image-based PDFs.

title: Data persistence (TDD)
description: Tests first. Store paystub records by person (first name) and tax year. Store filing status per year. Cache tax bracket data by year. Handle duplicate paystub uploads gracefully (same person, same pay period).

title: Tax bracket data (TDD)
description: Tests first. Fetch federal brackets + standard deductions from IRS sources, cache per year in SQLite, fallback to hardcoded 2025/2026 brackets. Support filing statuses: Single, MFJ, MFS, HoH.

title: Web UI (htmx + Go templates)
description: Upload page for paystubs. Filing status config per tax year. Dashboard with withholding recommendation. Visualizations: tax bracket explorer, withholding trend over time. Supplemental income prompt with materiality threshold.
